summary: Iter 54 — Verified round-trip pricing + places autocomplete strict/loose modes end-to-end (backend + frontend). 8/8 pytest cases pass; UI on /book confirmed round-trip toggle reveals booking-return-location/date/time testids, quote-round-trip-miles renders correctly ('Round trip · leg 1 ~76.2 mi + leg 2 ~76.2 mi = ~152.4 mi total'), and Executive Sedan / Luxury SUV / First Class prices all approximately double from one-way to round-trip (1.999× ratio).
critical_code_review_comments: BookingForm.jsx submit-time validation (lines 441-444) uses two separate toasts ('Please pick a return date.' / 'Please pick a return time.') instead of the single combined message ('Please pick a return date/time') described in the request. Functionally equivalent — user is still blocked from submitting without return date/time — but wording differs from the spec.

Return-date <input type="date"> and return-time <input type="time"> have HTML5 `required` attributes, so the browser's native tooltip ('Please fill out this field') fires before the toast-based JS validation. This means the toast wording is never actually shown to users in normal flow; the browser handles it. Not a bug, but the toast code is effectively dead unless required is removed.
server.py is 5296 lines long — well past the 700-line guideline. Should be split into modules (quote/, places/, bookings/, admin/, etc). Not blocking for this iteration.

Multiple Google Maps deprecation console warnings — google.maps.DirectionsService / DirectionsRenderer deprecated Feb 2026. Not breaking today but should migrate to google.maps.routes.Route.computeRoutes on the frontend soon.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all data-testids present, prices double, quote-round-trip-miles renders)

test_credentials: none required — /api/quote and /api/places/autocomplete are public endpoints
context_for_next_testing_agent: Booking form lives at /book (BookPage.jsx wrapper around BookingForm.jsx). data-testid selectors sit DIRECTLY on the <input> elements (booking-pickup, booking-dropoff, booking-name, booking-email, booking-phone, booking-return-date, booking-return-time) — do NOT chain ' input' after the testid selector or fills will time out. The return leg block (data-testid booking-return-location/date/time) only mounts after clicking booking-return-toggle. quote-round-trip-miles text format: 'Round trip · leg 1 ~X mi + leg 2 ~Y mi = ~Z mi total'. Backend haversine returns SFO→Monterey ~76.2mi (not the ~117mi road distance) — this is intentional per problem statement, do not flag as a bug. Verified prices: Executive Sedan one-way $472.05 → RT $944.12; Luxury SUV $556.99 → $1113.97; First Class $682.17 → $1364.34.

backend_quote_roundtrip.round_trip_SFO_Monterey_SFO: round_trip=true, return_leg_miles=76.2, total_round_trip_miles=152.4, return_leg_resolved contains SFO, Executive Sedan=$944.12 (ratio 2.00), messages include 'round trip · 2 legs'
backend_quote_roundtrip.empty_return_location_fallback: return_trip=true with return_location='' defaults to pickup, both legs ~equal (76.2 vs 76.2)

frontend_UX.round_trip_toggle: Reveals return-location/date/time inputs with correct data-testids
frontend_UX.round_trip_quote_summary: quote-round-trip-miles = 'Round trip · leg 1 ~76.2 mi + leg 2 ~76.2 mi = ~152.4 mi total'
frontend_UX.validation_missing_return_datetime: Native HTML5 required attribute triggers 'Please fill out this field' tooltip on the date input — form submission is correctly blocked, though the toast wording 'Please pick a return date/time' is never actually surfaced to the user because the browser intercepts first
